channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Anistia a Bolsonaro, terras raras, combate ao crime: as propostas de Caiado no 1º discurso
item.description: Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) of Goiás launched his pre-candidacy for the 2026 Brazilian presidency, proposing as his first act a broad, general, and unrestricted amnesty for Jair Bolsonaro and others involved in institutional tensions, aiming to pacify the country. He also advocated for Brazil to lead in the exploration and processing of critical minerals like heavy rare earths through partnerships with the U.S. and Japan, and pledged to combat organized crime by integrating state and federal police forces.

item.title: PSD anuncia Caiado como pré-candidato à Presidência
item.description: The PSD announced Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as its pre-candidate for the 2026 Brazilian presidential election, following the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior and the sidelining of Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite. Caiado will resign as governor on March 31, 2026, to focus on the campaign, with his vice-governor Daniel Vilela (MDB) succeeding him. In his announcement speech, Caiado denounced political polarization as an artificial construct benefiting those in power, asserting that Brazil can no longer endure the current climate.

item.title: Caiado vê vitória sobre Lula como provável, mas alerta para desafio de governar
item.description: Former Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado said defeating Lula in the 2026 elections would be easy, but warned that governing the country would be the real challenge. To counter polarization, he promised that, if elected, his first act would be to grant a general and unrestricted amnesty. He criticized the current administration and stressed the need to restore governability.

item.title: Caiado diz que anistiará Bolsonaro e outros golpistas se ganhar a eleição
item.description: Goiás governor and PSD presidential pre-candidate Ronaldo Caiado announced he would grant a broad, general, and unrestricted amnesty to those involved in the January 8 coup attempt, including Jair Bolsonaro, as his first act if elected. He framed the measure as a way to pacify Brazil and overcome political polarization. The statement was made during the launch of his pre-candidacy in São Paulo, following Ratinho Jr.'s withdrawal and the party's decision led by Gilberto Kassab.

item.title: Crusoé: Caiado promete anistia para acabar com polarização. Vai colar?
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, former governor of Goiás, announced his presidential candidacy for 2026 under the PSD, positioning himself as a third way to counter the polarization between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro. He proposed amnesty as a key measure to reduce political conflict in Brazil. The strategy aims to attract voters dissatisfied with the two dominant ideological poles.

item.title: Eduardo e Michelle rebatem Moraes após pedido de explicações sobre vídeo
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro and former first lady Michelle Bolsonaro rebuked Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes after he demanded explanations regarding a video Eduardo claimed to have shown to Jair Bolsonaro, who is barred from using cell phones and social media. They accused Moraes of censorship and judicial persecution against the Bolsonaro family. The incident unfolds amid ongoing tensions between the former president’s allies and the STF.

item.title: PL pede ao STF que próximo presidente da Alerj assuma interinamente o governo do Rio de Janeiro
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) asked the Supreme Federal Court (STF) to have the next president of the Rio de Janeiro State Legislative Assembly (Alerj) assume the governorship on an interim basis, challenging Minister Cristiano Zanin’s decision to appoint the president of the Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice as governor. The PL claims the decision is unconstitutional and that the TJ-RJ president should only act as a last-resort successor while the Alerj president is unable to serve. The petition follows the preventive detention of Rodrigo Bacellar (former Alerj president) and the conviction of Cláudio Castro for abuse of power in the 2022 elections.

item.title: Kassab decreta o fim da alternativa a Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, leader of the PSD, ended the expectation that his party could serve as a moderate alternative between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in the 2026 elections. With Ratinho Junior’s definitive withdrawal from the race, the PSD’s project of institutional responsibility collapsed, reinforcing polarization. The article states that Kassab, as a national political strategist, no longer sees room for a viable third way.

item.title: Caiado promete anistia a Bolsonaro em 1º discurso após escolha do PSD
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás and PSD's presidential pre-candidate, advocated for 'deactivation' of political polarization in his first speech after the party's nomination, but promised a 'broad, general and unrestricted amnesty' for Jair Bolsonaro and others convicted in the 2022 coup attempt as his first government act. He positioned himself as an alternative to both PT and Bolsonarism, emphasizing his agribusiness background, technological innovation, and public security focus, stating that 'criminals are not made' in Goiás and that drug trafficking controls part of Brazil's territory.

item.title: Ibaneis deixa governo do DF para disputar o Senado em meio à crise do BRB e do Banco Master
item.description: Distrito Federal Governor Ibaneis Rocha (MDB) resigned to run for the Brazilian Senate in 2026, handing power to his vice-governor, Celina Leão (PP). His departure coincides with a growing scandal involving the BRB, which purchased R$ 12.2 billion in toxic loans from Daniel Vorcaro’s Banco Master, later identified by the Central Bank as lacking collateral. Ibaneis is under suspicion of knowing about the poor quality of the assets and has sought a R$ 4 billion FGC loan, using public assets as collateral, to bail out the BRB.

item.title: PSD oficializa pré-candidatura de Ronaldo Caiado à Presidência
item.description: The PSD has officially launched the pre-candidacy of Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado for the Brazilian presidency, following the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior. Caiado advocated for a broad amnesty for those convicted in connection with the January 8 events, citing Juscelino Kubitschek as a model, and stated his goal is to pacify Brazil. He previously ran for president in 1989 and has served as senator, federal deputy, and two-term governor of Goiás.

item.title: Lula pediu estudos para reduzir custo de crédito rotativo, diz Gleisi
item.description: Institutional Relations Minister Gleisi Hoffmann confirmed that President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva requested analyses from the Central Bank and the Ministry of Finance to reduce the cost of credit card revolving debt. The goal is to study ways to lower the high interest rates associated with this form of debt, which significantly affects consumers.

item.title: A punição a Marçal por ter chamado Nunes de ‘tchutchuca do PCC’
item.description: The TSE rejected an appeal by influencer Pablo Marçal and upheld a R$ 30,000 fine for calling São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes a 'tchutchuca do PCC' during the 2024 election debate. The unanimous decision confirmed the TRE-SP's understanding of abuse of freedom of expression.

item.title: Caiado é confirmado pré-candidato pelo PSD e diz que primeiro ato na Presidência será anistia a Bolsonaro
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado, has been confirmed as the PSD’s pre-candidate for the 2026 presidential election. He stated that his first act as president would be to grant amnesty to former president Jair Bolsonaro, sparking significant political controversy.

item.title: ‘A gente não escolhe como servir na vida pública’, diz Alckmin ao ser perguntado sobre permanência como vice de Lula
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin stated that one does not choose how to serve in public life, emphasizing that public life chooses how one can serve. He resigned from his ministerial post this week to prepare for the October elections, possibly running for Senate, while President Lula praised his loyalty and competence but suggested Alckmin consult with Fernando Haddad to determine where he could be most effective — leaving open the possibility that Alckmin may not remain as his running mate.

item.title: PL pede para que novo presidente da Alerj seja governador do RJ até eleição-tampão
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) has asked the Supreme Federal Court (STF) to appoint the newly elected president of the Alerj as interim governor of Rio de Janeiro until the special election, following the cassation of state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar. The PL argues that, under the state constitution, the Alerj president is first in the line of succession, and that the current interim governor, Justice Ricardo Couto, may only serve until a legislative representative is in place. The TRE-RJ will reprocess the 2022 election votes on March 31 to determine Bacellar’s replacement.

item.title: “Eles têm problema na família”, diz Valdemar sobre os Bolsonaro
item.description: Valdemar da Costa Neto, president of the PL party, said the Bolsonaro family has internal problems that must be resolved for Flávio Bolsonaro to remain competitive in the 2026 election, emphasizing that the race will be decided by very narrow margins.

item.title: ACM Neto define chapa com ex-ministro de Bolsonaro e antigo membro da base de Jerônimo como nomes ao Senado na Bahia
item.description: ACM Neto has defined his major ticket for the 2026 Bahia gubernatorial race, naming João Roma (former Bolsonaro minister and PL-BA president) and Senator Angelo Coronel (ex-PSD, now independent) as Senate candidates, with Zé Cocá (PP) as running mate for vice-governor. The ticket aims to unite Bolsonarist support and dissidents from Governor Jerônimo Rodrigues’ base, though formal negotiations with Flávio Bolsonaro for mutual presidential support remain pending.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula realiza entrega simbólica de 107 obras de educação
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva symbolically delivered 107 education works nationwide on Monday, March 30, 2026, celebrating the achievement of internet access in 99,000 public schools for pedagogical use. The event, held at the Ministry of Education with Ministers Camilo Santana (Education) and Frederico de Siqueira Filho (Communications), highlighted a federal investment of R$ 8.8 billion in the National Connected Schools Strategy, with R$ 6.5 billion from the New PAC. The delivered projects include daycare centers, full-time schools, federal institutes, universities, and university hospitals.

item.title: Empresário é condenado por dizer para um funcionário ‘fazer o L’ ao ser cobrado por salário atrasado
item.description: Brazil's Superior Labor Court upheld the conviction of a Fortaleza businessman who was ordered to pay 10,000 reais in damages to an employee for political harassment, including telling him to 'make the L' when demanding overdue wages and claiming his son's robbery was deserved for voting for Lula. The ruling affirmed that the remarks violated human dignity and political freedom of conviction, dismissing the defense's claims of informality and mutual political expression.

item.title: Pesquisa anima time de Haddad e estimula sentimento de ‘virada possível’
item.description: A recent Atlas/Estadão poll shows Tarcísio de Freitas leading with 49.1% voter intention versus 42.6% for Fernando Haddad, but Haddad’s allies celebrate the result as a sign that an upset is possible, noting Haddad only recently entered the race and the campaign has not yet begun to expose the governor’s weaknesses.

item.title: Proposta sobre diesel avança nos Estados, mas anúncio sai na 3ª feira
item.description: The Comsefaz advanced discussions on a proposal to mitigate diesel price increases, with an announcement scheduled for Tuesday, March 31, 2026. The Lula government's proposal includes subsidies for diesel imports, reducing the price by R$1.20 per liter, at a total cost of R$3 billion over two months, shared between the federal and state governments. Some states requested additional time to review the agreement due to Fiscal Responsibility Law constraints.

item.title: Valdemar descarta Michelle como vice de Flávio e explica motivo
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the PL, ruled out Michelle Bolsonaro as a potential vice-presidential candidate on Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) 2026 ticket, stating that the spot should be open to candidates from other parties, especially from the Northeast. He emphasized that the final decision rests with Flávio and Jair Bolsonaro, and that he will not interfere due to his internal party responsibilities. Despite advocating for a woman on the ticket to attract female voters, he repeatedly rejected Michelle’s name, saying he has already answered 'no' at least 13 times.

item.title: Reação de Moraes a vídeo de Eduardo Bolsonaro eleva tensão com STF
item.description: Minister Alexandre de Moraes ordered Bolsonaro's defense to explain, within 24 hours, a video where Eduardo Bolsonaro claims he is showing content to his father, who is barred from using social media. The measure reignites the conflict between the former president and the STF, with the risk of Bolsonaro returning to closed regime at Papuda if violations are proven.

item.title: Valdemar diz que Flávio terá de mostrar programa e torce por vice mulher na chapa do PL
item.description: PL president Valdemar Costa Neto said Senator Flávio Bolsonaro must present a concrete plan and stop wasting time attacking President Lula. He also expressed hope that Flávio chooses a woman as his running mate for the party's 2026 presidential ticket.

item.title: Lula e Alcolumbre devem discutir nesta semana a indicação de Messias ao STF
item.description: President Lula is set to meet this week with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre to discuss the nomination of Attorney General Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court. The meeting aims to overcome institutional resistance and formally submit the nomination after Senate criticism over delays. Allies of the AGU claim to have secured at least 60 favorable votes, surpassing the 41 required for approval.

item.title: Valdemar diz que família Bolsonaro tem de 'resolver problemas' para poder vencer as eleições
item.description: Valdemar da Costa Neto, president of the PL, said the Bolsonaro family must resolve its internal problems to win the 2026 elections, emphasizing that family unity is crucial for Eduardo Bolsonaro's return to Brazil and Flávio Bolsonaro's presidential bid. He defended Eduardo after the STF questioned a video recorded in the U.S., stating Eduardo did not lie but sought to communicate with his father, and noted that Jair Bolsonaro has always respected judicial measures.

item.title: O papel de Tereza Cristina na campanha de Flávio, segundo Valdemar
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the PL, stated that Senator and former Agriculture Minister Tereza Cristina will not be the vice-presidential candidate on Flávio Bolsonaro’s 2026 ticket, as she intends to remain in the Federal Senate. Although not running as vice, she will assist in developing Flávio’s government plan. Valdemar had previously favored Tereza Cristina for the ticket, believing her inclusion would strengthen support from the agribusiness sector and the União-PP federation.

item.title: A segunda disputa de Ronaldo Caiado pela Presidência
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado is making his second presidential run, after receiving less than 1% of the vote in 1989. In 2026, he is betting on a 'moderate right-wing' discourse to attract voters dissatisfied with both Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, positioning himself as a center-right alternative.

item.title: Aliados veem Haddad competitivo e apontam recuo no ‘já ganhou’ de Tarcísio em SP
item.description: The Atlas/Estadão poll shows Tarcísio de Freitas at 49.1% and Fernando Haddad at 42.6% in voter intentions for the São Paulo gubernatorial race in 2026, narrowing the governor's lead. PT allies now view Haddad as more competitive than initially expected, while Tarcísio's supporters are showing less certainty about an easy win. The Lula administration sees room for Haddad to grow and force a runoff, given São Paulo's electoral weight in national politics.

item.title: Inelegível, Arruda é o pré-candidato ao governo do DF que mais gasta em impulsionamento
item.description: José Roberto Arruda (PSD), still ineligible and awaiting an STF decision, is the DF gubernatorial pre-candidate who spent the most on digital boosting in the last 90 days, according to an exclusive report. His digital outreach investment exceeds that of other pre-candidates, despite his current inability to run for office.

item.title: Michelle nega envio de vídeo de Eduardo a Bolsonaro e tenta afastar suspeita de burla a decisão de Moraes
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro denied that any videos recorded by deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro were sent or shown to former president Jair Bolsonaro, following Eduardo’s remarks at CPAC where he said he was recording a video to show his father. The statement aims to dispel suspicions of violating Justice Alexandre de Moraes’ house arrest order, which bans any external communication, including through third parties. Michelle emphasized that all conditions are being fully complied with.

item.title: Caiado foi escolhido como pré-candidato por ter mais chances de chegar no segundo turno, diz Kassab
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD, announced that Ronaldo Caiado was chosen as the party's pre-candidate for the presidency due to his higher chances of reaching the runoff in the 2026 elections. Kassab believes Caiado could defeat either Lula or Flávio Bolsonaro in the second round. He also praised Eduardo Leite and Ratinho Júnior but emphasized the decision was based on electoral viability, while criticizing Lula and Bolsonaro's administrations for lacking reforms and mishandling the pandemic.

item.title: Deputada dos EUA critica Lula e fala em derrota do petista
item.description: U.S. Representative Maria Elvira Salazar (Republican-Florida) criticized Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in an X post on March 26, 2026, stating that 'Brazilians can no longer tolerate Lula' and predicting his defeat in the 2026 elections. She accused Lula of aligning with Latin American dictators, defending criminals, governing through a 'playbook' of censorship and persecution of opponents, and attempting to silence digital voices under the guise of defending democracy. Salazar concluded by asserting that 'Brazil will not retreat,' that 'freedom will prevail,' and that Lula will be defeated at the ballot box.

item.title: Congresso de prefeitos em SP chama presidenciáveis; Flávio confirma presença
item.description: The Municipal Congress in São Paulo, scheduled for April 6–8, has invited President Lula and the main pre-candidates for the 2026 presidency. Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) confirmed attendance, with Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) and Romeu Zema (Novo) also expected. The event serves as a platform for dialogue between municipal leaders and national figures in an election year.

item.title: A reação de Valdemar à pré-candidatura de Ronaldo Caiado ao Planalto
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL party, expressed doubts about Ronaldo Caiado's pre-candidacy for president under the PSD, stating that the 2026 election runoff will be between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro. He suggested Caiado, despite being right-wing, will ultimately support Bolsonaro's son, and urged all right-wing candidates to unite behind Flávio in the first round to secure victory over Lula.

item.title: PL entra com pedido no STF para reivindicar que futuro presidente da Alerj assuma como governador interino
item.description: The PL filed an amicus curiae brief with the STF to ensure that the future president of Alerj, Douglas Ruas, assumes as interim governor, upholding the constitutional succession line over the interim appointment of Judge Ricardo Couto ordered by Minister Cristiano Zanin. The party argues that Article 141 of the state constitution establishes succession through the Legislative Assembly, and that the debate over the direct election format for the interim mandate should not override the interim rule. The STF will review the PSD’s direct election action on April 8.

item.title: Deputados propõem lei que pode classificar como antissemita quem faz críticas a Israel
item.description: A bill proposed in Brazil's Chamber of Deputies seeks to define antisemitism based on the IHRA working definition, potentially allowing criticism of Israel to be classified as antisemitic depending on interpretation. Supported by lawmakers across the political spectrum, including Tabata Amaral and Kim Kataguiri, the bill does not create new crimes but links the issue to the Racism Law, which could affect how existing legislation is applied. Authors state the goal is to clarify public policy, not to restrict political debate within constitutional limits.

item.title: Futuro da candidatura do Missão é o fiel da balança das chances de Tarcísio vencer no 1º turno em SP
item.description: Kim Kataguiri's candidacy for the Missão party could be decisive in the São Paulo 2026 election. If he withdraws or performs weakly, his voters are likely to shift largely to Tarcísio de Freitas, boosting the governor's chances of winning in the first round. If he maintains a strong candidacy, especially among young voters (where he has 23.7% support), he could siphon crucial votes from the governor and push the race to a runoff.

item.title: Sucessão no Rio: PL tenta derrubar decisão que preteriu presidente da Alerj do governo interino
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) has asked the Supreme Federal Court (STF) to overturn Minister Cristiano Zanin's decision maintaining Rio de Janeiro State Justice Tribunal President Ricardo Couto as interim governor. The PL contends that the state constitution mandates that the future president of the Legislative Assembly (Alerj) automatically assume the governorship, and seeks to install deputy Douglas Ruas in that role to serve as interim governor until the extraordinary election. The party argues the STF ruling violates constitutional succession rules by favoring a judicial official lacking popular legitimacy over an elected legislator.

item.title: Kassab explica escolha de Caiado
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD, announced that Ronaldo Caiado was selected as the party's presidential candidate for 2026 due to his higher electoral chances. The decision followed an internal contest with Rio Grande do Sul governor Eduardo Leite. Kassab stressed the choice was based on electoral viability assessments.

item.title: Moraes dá 24h para Bolsonaro explicar fala de Eduardo sobre gravação de vídeo para o pai
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes gave Bolsonaro's defense 24 hours to explain a statement by deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro regarding a video recorded for his father. The order is part of investigations into potential abuse of power and misinformation. Eduardo's comment suggested the video could serve as evidence if something happened to the ex-president.

item.title: Michelle nega ter recebido vídeo de Eduardo
item.description: First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro denied receiving a video from former deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro, who claimed he had recorded the material to show to his father, Jair Bolsonaro, during CPAC in the U.S. Eduardo's statement prompted Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes to order Bolsonaro's defense to explain a possible violation of house arrest measures. Moraes considers the remarks may constitute a breach of the imposed restrictions.

item.title: Análise: Ao se equilibrar entre os 'valores do Brasil profundo' e o gestor moderno, Caiado tem tarefa quixotesca em eleição polarizada por Lula e Flávio
item.description: Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD-GO), aged 76, faces a highly polarized presidential election between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, attempting to balance his image as a modern manager with appeal to the 'values of Brazil's heartland'. His profile as an 'obstinate aspirant of the second way of the right' highlights the challenges of positioning himself in a landscape dominated by two antagonistic figures, where his candidacy is seen as quixotic due to the difficulty of gaining traction in a deeply divided electorate.

item.title: Lula manda estudar novos limites para juros do rotativo
item.description: President Lula has directed the economic team to study new limits on credit card revolving interest rates, which currently reach 435.9% per year, comparing the modality to the overdraft facility, which already has a fixed rate of 8%. Although a rule exists capping interest at 100% of the debt (limiting total to R$2,000 for a R$1,000 debt), it has not been effectively implemented, allowing revolving credit to remain the most expensive credit line in the country. The goal is to reduce household indebtedness, given that 78.9% of families were in debt by the end of 2025 and 73.3 million Brazilians were delinquent in January 2026.

item.title: Geração autocracia cresceu com esquerda no poder e busca direita radical como solução
item.description: Brazilian youth aged 16 to 24 show growing support for the radical right, led by Renan Santos of the Missão party and Flávio Bolsonaro, with voting intentions reaching up to 37% in this age group. This movement, fueled by anti-state and religious rhetoric, seeks gradual autocratization through the erosion of fundamental rights, despite formally defending electoral democracy. The AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll reveals a pronounced gender gap, with men significantly more likely to support these candidacies.

item.title: A preferência de Valdemar para a vice de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the PL, said he is 'rooting' for Flávio Bolsonaro to choose a woman as his running mate in 2026, claiming women are 'much better' than men in every way. He also criticized the 2022 choice of Walter Braga Netto as vice president, stating the general brought no votes to Bolsonaro and suggested Tereza Cristina would have been a better pick.

item.title: Gleisi diz que Lula pediu estudos sobre rotativo do cartão de crédito em meio a preocupação com endividamento das famílias
item.description: Minister Gleisi Hoffmann revealed that President Lula requested studies from the Central Bank and the Ministry of Finance to lower credit card revolving interest rates, which he described as unjustifiably high. Lula linked household debt from online purchases of cheap goods to public frustration blamed on the government, while affirming that debt for asset-building is acceptable.

item.title: Governo Tarcísio inicia audiências sobre concessão de Complexo do Ibirapuera e Vila Olímpica
item.description: The government of Tarcísio de Freitas held public hearings in April to present a proposal for the concession of the Ibirapuera Sports Complex and Vila Olímpica Mário Covas to private entities, aiming to improve infrastructure and expand public access to sports and leisure activities. The project, previously attempted by governors João Doria (2019) and Geraldo Alckmin (2017), faces opposition due to the site’s heritage status granted by Iphan in 2024, following an earlier proposal that included demolition and commercial development such as shopping centers and modern arenas.

item.title: Gleisi diz que PSD preferiu “figura mais agressiva” com Caiado
item.description: Gleisi Hoffmann stated that the PSD's choice of Ronaldo Caiado as its 2026 presidential candidate increases electoral polarization, as he is seen as a more aggressive figure than Eduardo Leite. She argued this leaves little room for a competitive third way and commented on the political situation in Paraná, where she will run for Senate. Gleisi also noted that the SRI will be temporarily led by its executive secretary until a permanent appointment is made.
